column5: Last update — June 5, 2025 I've been fascinated with Wesley's visual style for as long as I can remember (it started with whenever I watched Mr. Fox for the first time as a kid) and had I really dug into his work a bit earlier than when I did, I think I would hold him in the same regard as someone like Tarantino, Kubrick, or Paul Thomas Anderson (relative to what my tastes were when I was 16). His ability to develop strong characters primarily from their mannerisms and odd behaviors is truly endearing and very similar to how I get to know people in my own life. He is in full control of every world he creates and I don't want it any other fucking way! I don't think I'll ever dislike one of his films or be anything short of fascinated by what he puts out.
